scenario: A DMN decision table has an "Annotation" column showing free-text comments (e.g., "Applies to EU customers per regulation X"). The team wonders if annotations affect evaluation logic.
question: Annotation column purpose -- does it affect decision evaluation?

options.text: Annotation columns in a DMN decision table affect evaluation logic -- for example, a rule annotated with "EU customers only" would cause the engine to skip that rule for non-EU inputs. This misunderstands the role of annotations. Annotations are purely human-readable documentation added to rule rows. The DMN engine reads only the input condition cells and output cells when evaluating rules; the annotation column content plays no role in determining matches or outputs during decision execution.

Annotations don't affect evaluation logic -- they're documentation for human readers (modelers, reviewers, auditors). The engine ignores annotations when evaluating rules. Useful for: documenting regulatory basis ("per GDPR Article X"), explaining edge-case rules ("rule for legacy customers, sunset 2030"), audit trails (who added the rule, when, why). Best practice: rich annotations in production DMN improve maintainability + compliance review. 3 spec feature; the Camunda 8 docs at docs/components/modeler/dmn/ do not enumerate them as a first-class C8 modeler feature (0 grep hits for annotation / Annotation), so they're spec-supported but documented at the OMG DMN standard level rather than in C8-specific guides. dmn XML.

Annotation text is evaluated by the engine for the first matching rule only, giving modelers a way to attach conditional comments to rules. This attributes behaviour to annotations that they do not have. Annotations exist across all rule rows equally and are evaluated by neither the first nor any match -- they are ignored by the engine in all hit policies (UNIQUE, FIRST, RULE ORDER, COLLECT, ANY). Their sole purpose is to communicate rule intent to human readers reviewing the decision model.

Annotations are a proprietary Camunda extension and are not part of the official DMN standard. 3 specification as a standard element of decision table syntax. They appear as an additional column in decision tables in both the DMN specification and in tooling that supports it. Camunda 8 Web Modeler and Desktop Modeler render annotation columns in accordance with the DMN standard without needing any custom extensions.

a.text: Incorrect. Annotation columns in DMN decision tables: What they are: Free-text columns added to rule rows. Modeler-defined content; arbitrary strings. Per-rule: each rule can have annotation text. What the engine does with them: Ignores during evaluation: doesn't read for inputs or outputs. May include in output trace / evaluation result: some engines return annotations alongside outputs (for debugging / audit). Practical uses: Regulatory
